applied_optimizations.name: conv2d equivalence transformation
applied_optimizations.description: Transformed ConvTranspose2d to equivalent F.conv2d with flipped weights and padding=kernel_size-1 for stride=1 case. This leverages better MIOpen optimization for regular convolution.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile with inductor
attempted_but_failed.reason: MLIR compilation errors with async operations on AMD MI355X (gfx950)
attempted_but_failed.error: failed to legalize operation 'ttg.async_copy_global_to_local' that was explicitly marked illegal

attempted_but_failed.name: Manual Triton kernel with 3D grid
attempted_but_failed.reason: HIP invalid argument errors and type consistency issues in Triton loops
attempted_but_failed.error: Loop-carried variable acc has initial type inconsistent

attempted_but_failed.name: Environment variable optimizations
attempted_but_failed.reason: PYTORCH_TUNABLEOP_ENABLED and TORCH_BLAS_PREFER_HIPBLASLT actually slowed down performance

top_bottlenecks: conv2d GEMM operation dominates execution time

notes: Transposed convolution with stride=1, padding=0 is mathematically equivalent to regular conv2d with flipped weights and padding=kernel_size-1
PyTorch/ROCm MIOpen already highly optimizes this operation - difficult to achieve significant speedups
torch.compile causes MLIR errors on AMD MI355X (gfx950) with certain Triton async operations
Best achieved: 5.51ms vs 5.49ms baseline (0.996x speedup, score=50)
The operation is memory-bound for this configuration - compute is already well-optimized
